ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon (GhostEmperor/Earth Estries) Exchange/IIS web-shell post-exploit chain: w3wp child shell → LSASS/NTDS credential access
ucs.description: Hunts Salt Typhoon's signature edge-exploitation playbook against Microsoft Exchange and IIS: ProxyLogon/ProxyShell or similar T1190 abuse drops a web shell that w3wp.exe spawns reconnaissance/credential-access children. Correlates a web-tier process spawning a shell with LSASS-touching or NTDS-extraction activity on the same host within a 10-minute window — the exact chain Trend Micro and Mandiant attribute to Earth Estries/Salt Typhoon at U.S. telcos and government targets.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as web_spawn_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe") Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe","quser.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe") by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process_name Processes.process_name Processes.process
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rename web_spawn_time as earliest_web_time
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as cred_time from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process IN ("*lsass*","*comsvcs.dll*MiniDump*","*MiniDumpWriteDump*","*SeDebugPrivilege*","*ntds.dit*","*ntdsutil*","*reg* save*HKLM\\SAM*","*reg* save*HKLM\\SYSTEM*","*reg* save*HKLM\\SECURITY*","*vssadmin*create shadow*","*procdump*lsass*")) by Processes.dest Processes.process_name Processes.process
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
    | rename process as cred_process process_name as cred_image
]
| where cred_time >= earliest_web_time AND cred_time <= earliest_web_time + 600
| eval delay_sec=cred_time-earliest_web_time, web_spawn_time=strftime(earliest_web_time,"%F %T"), cred_time=strftime(cred_time,"%F %T")
| table web_spawn_time cred_time delay_sec dest user parent_process_name process_name process cred_image cred_process
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 600;
// Step 1: web-tier process spawning shell / recon utilities (post-exploit indicator)
let WebShellSpawn = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe","httpd.exe","tomcat.exe","java.exe","UMWorkerProcess.exe")
    | where FileName in~ ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cscript.exe","wscript.exe",
                          "net.exe","net1.exe","whoami.exe","systeminfo.exe","tasklist.exe",
                          "quser.exe","nltest.exe","ipconfig.exe","hostname.exe","reg.exe","schtasks.exe")
    | project WebSpawnTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              WebParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ShellChild = FileName,
              ShellCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
// Step 2: credential-access activity on the same host
let CredAccess = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("lsass","MiniDumpWriteDump","SeDebugPrivilege","ntds.dit","ntdsutil"))
         or (FileName =~ "rundll32.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and ProcessCommandLine has "MiniDump")
         or (FileName in~ ("procdump.exe","procdump64.exe") and ProcessCommandLine has "lsass")
         or (FileName =~ "reg.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has "save" and ProcessCommandLine has_any (@"HKLM\SAM",@"HKLM\SYSTEM",@"HKLM\SECURITY"))
         or (FileName =~ "vssadmin.exe" and ProcessCommandLine has_all ("create","shadow"))
    | project CredTime = Timestamp, DeviceId,
              CredFile = FileName,
              CredCmd = ProcessCommandLine;
WebShellSpawn
| join kind=inner CredAccess on DeviceId
| where CredTime between (WebSpawnTime .. WebSpawnTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project WebSpawnTime, CredTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', CredTime, WebSpawnTime),
          DeviceName, AccountName, WebParent, ShellChild, ShellCmd, CredFile, CredCmd
| order by WebSpawnTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 7d;
let WindowSec = 600;
let WebShellSpawn = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where ParentProcessName endswith "\\w3wp.exe"
         or ParentProcessName endswith "\\httpd.exe"
         or ParentProcessName endswith "\\tomcat.exe"
         or ParentProcessName endswith "\\java.exe"
         or ParentProcessName endswith "\\UMWorkerProcess.exe"
    | where NewProcessName endswith_cs "\\cmd.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\powershell.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\pwsh.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\cscript.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\wscript.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\net.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\whoami.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\nltest.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\quser.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\tasklist.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\reg.exe"
         or tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\schtasks.exe"
    | project WebSpawnTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              WebParent = ParentProcessName,
              ShellChild = NewProcessName,
              ShellCmd = CommandLine;
let CredAccess = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | where Account !endswith "$"
    | where CommandLine has_any ("lsass","MiniDumpWriteDump","SeDebugPrivilege","ntds.dit","ntdsutil")
         or (tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\rundll32.exe" and CommandLine has "comsvcs.dll" and CommandLine has "MiniDump")
         or (tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\procdump.exe" and CommandLine has "lsass")
         or (tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\procdump64.exe" and CommandLine has "lsass")
         or (tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\reg.exe" and CommandLine has "save" and CommandLine has_any (@"HKLM\SAM",@"HKLM\SYSTEM",@"HKLM\SECURITY"))
         or (tolower(NewProcessName) endswith "\\vssadmin.exe" and CommandLine has_all ("create","shadow"))
    | project CredTime = TimeGenerated, Computer,
              CredFile = NewProcessName,
              CredCmd = CommandLine;
WebShellSpawn
| join kind=inner CredAccess on Computer
| where CredTime between (WebSpawnTime .. WebSpawnTime + WindowSec * 1s)
| project WebSpawnTime, CredTime,
          DelaySec = datetime_diff('second', CredTime, WebSpawnTime),
          Computer, Account, WebParent, ShellChild, ShellCmd, CredFile, CredCmd
| order by WebSpawnTime desc
ucs.rationale: Salt Typhoon's intrusions at U.S. telecommunications carriers, hospitality groups (FamousSparrow), and SE-Asia government targets (Earth Estries) consistently begin with edge-exposed Microsoft Exchange / IIS exploitation (ProxyLogon, ProxyShell, Log4Shell) → web shell → immediate hands-on-keyboard via w3wp-spawned shells → Mimikatz/comsvcs LSASS dumps or NTDS extraction → SMB/RDP lateral pivot. Pa

ucs.title: [LLM] Salt Typhoon Demodex/SparrowDoor/ShadowPad DLL side-loading via co-located signed binary
ucs.description: Hunts the GhostEmperor (Demodex rootkit), FamousSparrow (SparrowDoor), and Earth Estries (SnappyBee/ShadowPad/Crowdoor) hallmark: a legitimately-signed third-party AV/utility/AppData binary executed from a non-standard path (ProgramData, Public, Temp, PerfLogs) loading an adjacent unsigned DLL of the same vendor brand — Salt Typhoon's universal loader trick across all three clusters.
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("DTHelper.exe","K7AVMScn.exe","K7SysMon.exe","vmnat.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","vm3dservice.exe","BdReinit.exe","vftrace.exe","VFTRACE.exe","AvastSvc.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","sophosfilescanner.exe","javaw.exe","BgaSrv.exe","ESETSysInspector.exe")) AND (Processes.process_path IN ("*\\ProgramData\\*","*\\Users\\Public\\*","*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*","*\\AppData\\Roaming\\*","*\\PerfLogs\\*","*\\Windows\\Temp\\*","*\\Recycle*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| where NOT match(process_path, "^C:\\\\Program Files")
| eval firstTime=strftime(firstTime,"%F %T"), lastTime=strftime(lastTime,"%F %T")
| table firstTime lastTime dest user process_name process_path parent cmdline
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
// Signed loader binaries Salt Typhoon (Demodex/SparrowDoor/ShadowPad/SnappyBee) has been observed sideloading
let SaltTyphoonLoaders = dynamic([
    "dthelper.exe","k7avmscn.exe","k7sysmon.exe","vmnat.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","vm3dservice.exe",
    "bdreinit.exe","vftrace.exe","avastsvc.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","sophosfilescanner.exe",
    "javaw.exe","bgasrv.exe","esetsysinspector.exe","nv.exe","trillclient.exe"]);
let SuspiciousRoots = dynamic([
    @"c:\programdata\", @"c:\users\public\", @"c:\perflogs\",
    @"c:\windows\temp\", @"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\appdata\roaming\",
    @"$recycle.bin"]);
let Sideloaders = DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where tolower(FileName) in (SaltTyphoonLoaders)
    | extend FolderLower = tolower(FolderPath)
    | where FolderLower has_any (SuspiciousRoots)
    | where FolderLower !startswith @"c:\program files"
    | where FolderLower !startswith @"c:\windows\system32"
    | project LoaderTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              LoaderName = FileName, LoaderPath = FolderPath, LoaderSha256 = SHA256,
              LoaderCompany = ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName,
              LoaderCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              ParentName = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine;
let SideloadedDlls = DeviceImageLoadEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where FileName endswith ".dll"
    | extend DllFolderLower = tolower(FolderPath),
             HostFolderLower = tolower(InitiatingProcessFolderPath)
    | where DllFolderLower !startswith @"c:\windows\"
    | where DllFolderLower !startswith @"c:\program files"
    | where DllFolderLower == HostFolderLower               // DLL planted next to host
    | where tolower(InitiatingProcessFileName) in (SaltTyphoonLoaders)
    | project DllTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DllName = FileName, DllPath = FolderPath, DllSha256 = SHA256,
              DllSigner = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).Signer);
Sideloaders
| join kind=inner SideloadedDlls on DeviceId, $left.LoaderName == $right.InitiatingProcessFileName
| where DllTime between (LoaderTime - 2m .. LoaderTime + 2m)
| project LoaderTime, DeviceName, AccountName,
          LoaderName, LoaderPath, LoaderCompany, LoaderSha256,
          SideloadedDll = DllName, DllPath, DllSha256,
          ParentName, ParentCmd
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let SaltTyphoonLoaders = dynamic([
    "dthelper.exe","k7avmscn.exe","k7sysmon.exe","vmnat.exe","vmtoolsd.exe","vm3dservice.exe",
    "bdreinit.exe","vftrace.exe","avastsvc.exe","mcoemcpy.exe","sophosfilescanner.exe",
    "javaw.exe","bgasrv.exe","esetsysinspector.exe","nv.exe","trillclient.exe"]);
let SuspiciousRoots = dynamic([
    @"\programdata\", @"\users\public\", @"\perflogs\",
    @"\windows\temp\", @"\appdata\local\temp\", @"\appdata\roaming\",
    @"$recycle.bin"]);
let Sideloaders = SecurityEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where EventID == 4688
    | extend ImageLower = tolower(NewProcessName)
    | extend ImageBase = tostring(split(ImageLower, "\\")[-1])
    | where ImageBase in (SaltTyphoonLoaders)
    | where ImageLower has_any (SuspiciousRoots)
    | where not(ImageLower startswith @"c:\program files")
    | where not(ImageLower startswith @"c:\windows\system32")
    | project LoaderTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, Account,
              LoaderImage = NewProcessName,
              LoaderCmd = CommandLine,
              ParentImage = ParentProcessName;
// Sysmon EID 7 (image load) – populated when Sysmon is shipping into Sentinel
let SysmonLoads = WindowsEvent
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where Provider == "Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon"
    | where EventID == 7
    | extend Image = tostring(EventData.Image),
             ImageLoaded = tostring(EventData.ImageLoaded),
             Signed = tostring(EventData.Signed),
             Signature = tostring(EventData.Signature)
    | extend ImageLower = tolower(Image), DllLower = tolower(ImageLoaded)
    | extend ImageBase = tostring(split(ImageLower, "\\")[-1])
    | where ImageBase in (SaltTyphoonLoaders)
    | where DllLower endswith ".dll"
    | extend ImageDir = strcat_array(array_slice(split(ImageLower, "\\"), 0, -1), "\\")
    | extend DllDir   = strcat_array(array_slice(split(DllLower, "\\"),   0, -1), "\\")
    | where DllDir == ImageDir                              // DLL co-located with host
    | where Signed == "false" or Signature has_any ("", "-", "unsigned")
    | project DllTime = TimeGenerated, Computer, LoaderImageLower = ImageLower,
              SideloadedDll = ImageLoaded, DllSigned = Signed, DllSigner = Signature;
Sideloaders
| join kind=inner SysmonLoads on Computer
| where DllTime between (LoaderTime - 2m .. LoaderTime + 2m)
| project LoaderTime, DllTime, Computer, Account,
          LoaderImage, LoaderCmd, ParentImage,
          SideloadedDll, DllSigned, DllSigner
| order by LoaderTime desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: Salt Typhoon Signed Loader Executed from Non-Standard Path (Demodex/SparrowDoor/ShadowPad Side-Loading)
id: c5a31f0a-7c4b-4d2a-9c7e-9a4f1d31be77
status: experimental
description: Detects execution of signed third-party AV/utility binaries that Salt Typhoon (GhostEmperor/FamousSparrow/Earth Estries) is documented to abuse for DLL side-loading, when launched from suspicious roots (ProgramData, Public, AppData\Temp, PerfLogs, Recycle Bin).
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G1045/
  - https://www.trendmicro.com/en_us/research/24/k/earth-estries.html
  - https://securelist.com/ghostemperor-from-proxylogon-to-kernel-mode/104407/
author: SOC Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/13
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\DTHelper.exe'
      - '\K7AVMScn.exe'
      - '\K7SysMon.exe'
      - '\vmnat.exe'
      - '\vmtoolsd.exe'
      - '\vm3dservice.exe'
      - '\BdReinit.exe'
      - '\vftrace.exe'
      - '\AvastSvc.exe'
      - '\mcoemcpy.exe'
      - '\sophosfilescanner.exe'
      - '\javaw.exe'
      - '\BgaSrv.exe'
      - '\ESETSysInspector.exe'
      - '\nv.exe'
      - '\TrillClient.exe'
  selection_path:
    Image|contains:
      - '\ProgramData\'
      - '\Users\Public\'
      - '\AppData\Local\Temp\'
      - '\AppData\Roaming\'
      - '\PerfLogs\'
      - '\Windows\Temp\'
      - '$Recycle.Bin'
  filter_legit:
    Image|startswith:
      - 'C:\Program Files\'
      - 'C:\Program Files (x86)\'
      - 'C:\Windows\System32\'
  condition: selection_image and selection_path and not filter_legit
fields:
  - Image
  - ParentImage
  - CommandLine
  - User
falsepositives:
  - Genuine installer staging directories (rare for these vendors outside Program Files)
  - Portable AV tools intentionally launched from removable media
level: high
tags:
  - attack.defense-evasion
  - attack.persistence
  - attack.t1574.002
  - attack.t1036.005
  - detection.threat-hunting
ucs.rationale: Side-loading a malicious DLL into a signed third-party binary is the single technique that ties all three Salt Typhoon clusters together: Kaspersky's GhostEmperor Demodex dropper sideloads into K7 AV and Bitdefender binaries (BdReinit.exe, K7AVMScn.exe); ESET's FamousSparrow SparrowDoor sideloads into K7AVMScn.exe and Indusoft binaries; Trend Micro's Earth Estries reporting on SnappyBee/Crowdoor/S
